methodology: Composite 0-100 = momentum 20% + commodity-alignment 20% + deep-dive 15% + analyst 15% + TipRanks SmartScore 10% + news sentiment 10% + quality 10% (missing sub-scores redistribute). High = long candidate, low = short candidate. Sources: IBKR, FMP, TipRanks, Bigdata.com (RavenPack), Carbon Arc, MetalMiner.
macro: Hawkish Fed (64% Sept hike priced), strong dollar (DXY ~100.8), US-Iran conflict volatility with 60-day peace talks; metals complex broadly correcting after H1 melt-up. Q4 consensus: dollar downtrend -> possible precious rebound window in Q3/Q4.

commodityBias.evidence: US HRC >$1,100/st after Q4-25/Q1-26 rebound; 50% S232 tariffs keep imports down 26% YoY; supply tight (outages, lead times); NUE +23% in 3M; Cato/AISI: market likely to stay tight. China HC futures stabilizing/rebounding. Risk: PMI-driven demand softness. | MetalMiner: HRC buy 3-5mo ahead (Jun MMO); Jul: futures sideways = near-term ceiling risk, imports highest since Jul-25; HRC $1,124/st (+3.6% Jun), CRC $1,338 & HDG $1,404 broke resistance (buy 0-3mo); Nucor CSP $1,130; S/R: HRC 1,078/1,207

Rides carbon-steel tariff regime + grid/transformer demand; no direct price series. | MetalMiner: GOES sideways range, lower half — cover near-term + layer on dips; Baosteel hiked GOES +200-300 RMB/mt; transformer/grid demand constructive

War premium unwound after US-Iran MoU: LME fell from >$3,700 to ~$3,100-3,200; June -16% = worst month since 2008; China/Indonesia supply expansions restoring balance; range-bound $3,300-3,400 cap. LME still +45% YoY (producers' Q results strong). | MetalMiner: Jul MMO: 'purchase as needed' (downgraded from Jun 'buy ahead 0-3mo'); LME -15.55% Jun to $3,112/mt; drivers flipped negative (Iran resolution, Novelis Oswego return, rising USD); US A356 $2.914/lb flat, 26% of 1M range

LME ~$13.4K (+7.2% YTD, -8% off Jan record $14.5K); tight fundamentals (falling LME stocks, TC declining, concentrate shortage); AI/energy-transition demand; live two-sided catalyst = US refined-copper tariff decision (15% 2027/30% 2028) - confirmation bullish; sell-side 'buy dips', $15K targets. | MetalMiner: Closed above resistance -> buy 3-5mo ahead; MetalMiner shifting primary forecast to Comex; LME cash $13,356.5 (7/9), trend UP strength 2, bullish
GC -3% 1M, -14% 3M from $5,366 Feb peak; hawkish Fed + rate-hike bets + strong dollar; HSBC cut 2026/27 forecasts. Stabilizing early Q3; Q4 dollar-downtrend consensus = rebound risk to shorts.

SI $60.2: -11.5% 1M, -21% 3M, halved from Jan squeeze peak $115; same macro drivers as gold + industrial demand; extremely volatile, squeeze-prone. | MetalMiner: Comex spot $59.81, short-term UP strength 3 but only 14% of 1M range — bounce within downtrend

Platinum -21..-27% from Jan record after +125% 2025; UBS cut forecasts $300/oz, surplus risk YE; palladium in clear structural surplus (ICE substitution decline); consensus 'weak oscillation, lower H2 center'; pair idea in street research: long Pt / short Pd. PPLT -15% vs 200DMA, PALL -16%. | MetalMiner: Platinum sponge US $1,636/ozt flat, 30% of 1M range

Two-sided: carbonate +1/3 YTD, DoD $300M 5-yr stockpile tender, Zimbabwe export ban, 2026 supply growth < demand (tight balance thesis); BUT July futures weakening on CATL Jianxiawo restart + 3Mt/yr West Africa plant + slowing EV growth; carbonate-spodumene spreads normalizing. LIT -12.5% vs 50DMA after huge run. | MetalMiner: LiOH China €18,079.58/mt, trend DOWN strength 2, at bottom of 1M range — bearish near term; CBC weekly: avoid stockpiling before Jianxiawo output materializes
Follows lithium/nickel complex; Q3 restock (peak season) possible catalyst; KARS -11% vs 50DMA.

NdPr oxide avg +73.6% YoY in H1 (CNY 731K/t; metal CNY 940K/t); supply-demand gap widening from 2026 (CITIC); June PrNd output -6%, recycling share falling; demand from EVs, robots (Tesla Optimus), AC peak season, defense; ASX/US equities LAG the commodity (REMX -16% vs 50DMA) = catch-up long. Risk: 7-straight concentrate price rises just ended (Q3 -0.6% QoQ).

LME ~$16.5K; weak oscillation, stainless off-season, Indonesia quota decision (July window) is the swing factor; pessimism largely priced, bottoming, but upside capped unless macro turns; nickel-steel ratio at 3-yr low. | MetalMiner: LME 3M $16,535 flat, 17% of 1M range; stainless off-season, China port nickel-ore stocks record 4.735Mt, ferrochrome tender cuts -> bearish sentiment

Tin is the star: $53.3K, Myanmar/Wa State supply extremely inelastic, AI-server solder demand -> leading base-metal gains. Zinc $3.57-3.59K supported by tight mine supply, short-term bullish. Lead weak both ways. Group bias driven by tin. | MetalMiner: Tin LME $53,450 UP strength-2 bullish (67% of range); zinc China CNY24,810 at 100% of 1M range, supply tightening (Lady Loretta EOL -22.3kt, Kazzinc -13.8kt, Red Dog); lead below ST support -> buy dips only

Best-in-class US mini-mill riding HRC >$1,100/st with tariff-protected margins; +23% in 3M and analysts still see ~19% more. Being short the strongest name in a bullish commodity is a thesis conflict.

evidence.note: NUE +23% in 3M; HRC surged above $1,100/st, margins expanding
Nucor CSP hit $1,130/st on successive hikes; mills well-booked, buyers report limited leverage. Jul caveat: HRC futures sideways + imports rising = near-term ceiling risk

evidence.note: Copper tariff two-sided catalyst; LME $13.4K, tight
Copper closed above resistance -> 'buy 3-5 months ahead'; MetalMiner shifting primary forecasting to Comex (US price premium regime)

tickers.thesis: Brazil long steel; Buy consensus +25% PT, thin coverage (2).

Neutral consensus, +2% PT; QB2 issues largely known; Anglo merger completed backdrop.
Rebar/long-products play on tariffs + infrastructure with the best risk-reward in steel: Buy consensus (8/11), +28% to PT, and just beat on earnings (+4.2% on the day).

tickers.thesis: The US rare-earth champion: 11/11 buys, +49% to PT, NdPr +74% YoY with a widening supply gap, DoD price-floor deal, magnet ramp — and the stock is -48% off its high while the commodity keeps rising. Best new long in the universe.
risks: China export-policy reversals crushing NdPr price

evidence.note: NdPr oxide +73.6% YoY H1; supply gap widening from 2026

tickers.thesis: US heavy-RE magnet startup; 7/7 buys +146% PT but pre-revenue — high-octane RE satellite to MP.
Fabricator (aero/auto/packaging) that passes LME through — wrong instrument for a falling-LME short; Buy consensus +25% PT.
risks: for shorts: LME pass-through means falling aluminum HELPS margins

tickers.thesis: Sell consensus (5 sell/1 hold, zero buys), PT 14% BELOW price — the street thinks it's overvalued even in a bullish copper tape. Natural short leg against an FCX long (same commodity, valuation spread).
risks: premium valuation persists; copper melt-up lifts all

tickers.thesis: Uranium + REE separation optionality; Buy +71% PT.

Lowest-quartile-cost Brazilian spodumene: Q2 production 35kt beat guidance by 6%, 70% recovery, AISC $710/t falling to ~$620 with Phase 2, cash-flow positive even at $1,500/t. Strongest micro in the lithium complex.

evidence.note: CBC lithium weekly: avoid stockpiling before Jianxiawo output lands — spodumene price risk into Q3

evidence.note: LiOH China €18,079/mt at bottom of 1M range, trend down — near-term headwind to adds

tickers.thesis: Chemours: TiO2 bottoming (China prices +8% QTD) + fluoropolymers; Buy consensus +39% PT; litigation overhang known. Keep.

Largest gold major, Buy +48% PT; in a falling-gold tape prefer as future rebound long, not now.

As a lithium-complex short this leg is fighting the tape: record Q2 deliveries 480K (+25% YoY, beat by 74K), Carbon Arc US registrations accelerating every month (Jan 30.3K → May 47.4K), UBS raising PT with margin-surprise potential into July 22 earnings. Only works as a macro/valuation short — the micro is against it right now.
catalysts: Q2 earnings 2026-07-22 (auto GM est 19% vs consensus 17.6%)
risks: for shorts: earnings beat + FSD/robotaxi hype

recommendations.rationale: Thesis conflict: short the strongest US mill while HRC >$1,100 is tariff-tight and consensus is Buy +19%. -$21.8 uPnL says the tape agrees. Free the $684 of short gross for shorts that work.
Same conflict as NUE (Buy +16% PT). Between the two 'quality mill' shorts and RS/CLF, keep only the weak ones.
The one steel short the street endorses: Neutral, PT +0.5% — fully valued distributor. Serves as the group's valuation short.
Best steel short on micro (0 buys, negative EPS revisions, -23%/month) BUT July 23 earnings on a crowded short is binary. Trim 73→50 shares before 7/23, re-add on any squeeze.

Replaces part of the covered NUE/STLD short gross on the RIGHT side of the tariff: Canadian mill selling into a 50%-tariff US market, -23% below 50DMA. Keeps steel-group net roughly balanced (discovered name).

Record deliveries (+25% YoY), Carbon Arc US registrations accelerating 5 straight months, UBS sees margin beat. The EV-short is better expressed via the KARS ETF you already hold short — no single-stock earnings bomb.
Becomes the book's downstream-EV short after TSLA is covered; China-heavy, no earnings-date risk.
Redundant: overlaps LIT (both hold ALB/SQM — you are shorting your own longs twice). Keep one ETF hedge only; +$18 uPnL banked.
The single remaining lithium-complex hedge against ALB/SQM/SGML/LAC longs. Watch: it still holds your longs — it is a beta hedge, not alpha.
Zacks #1, Buy +71% PT, Q1 EBITDA +148% — but PT cuts continue; do not add until lithium futures stabilize (CATL restart pressure).
Neutral consensus but cost-curve winner; iodine cushions.
Strongest lithium micro: Q2 production beat +6%, AISC falling to $620/t, CF-positive at $1,500/t. Catalyst 8/14 financials.
Funded Thacker Pass option (DOE $2.23B, GM JV) but pre-revenue until 2028 and GS Neutral $4.50. Position is already 3.5% NAV — right size for an option.
Correct instrument for the aluminum-short bias (pure smelter leverage, LME war-premium unwound). Tiny at 0.4% NAV.
Higher-cost smelter, same falling-LME thesis.
Integrated so less pure, but consistent with the group bias.
Wrong instrument: a fabricator that PASSES THROUGH metal costs — falling LME helps its margins. Buy consensus +25%. The short contradicts the very thesis that justifies AA/CENX.

The book has ZERO copper exposure against a long-bias, tight-fundamentals copper market with the US tariff decision as a live catalyst. 12/15 buys, +17% PT. This is the single biggest gap in the book.
Street's only metals Sell consensus (PT -14%). Pairs the FCX long into a market-neutral copper spread if you want to keep net short unchanged.
11/11 buys, +49% PT, NdPr +74% YoY with widening supply gap, and RE equities lag the commodity. Best unheld long in the entire universe scan.
-42% YTD and still: loss-making palladium ops, high debt, restructuring — in a structurally-surplus palladium market.
Second PGM short; bias short with high confidence.
Pre-revenue developer in weak palladium — right thesis, but $169M micro-cap: any financing/partner headline squeezes. Do not add.
Chronic underperformer; note its copper JV (Cerro Verde) partially fights the short if copper rips.
Quality large-cap with Buy 5/2 and +56% PT — the wrong silver short. Keep the high-beta/high-cost trio AG/CDE/HL instead.
2-3x unhedged silver beta — the sharpest silver short while silver bias is down. Respect squeeze history (Jan $115).
Higher-cost Ag/Au beta short.
Completes the silver-beta short trio.
The book shorts silver/PGM but has no gold short while gold bias is down: HMY = high-cost SA gold, the classic gold-downside name (discovered, not in Excel).
6/6 buys but +2% PT — the street says fully priced. Ride the aero cycle, take profit if it stalls under $205 (PT high).
Buy +39% PT; TiO2 hikes sticking in China (+8% QTD).
Name-for-name TiO2 swap: TROX Neutral 1/5/1 + leverage vs KRO Zacks Buy, volumes +4%, +33%/yr vs industry -10%. Same commodity exposure, stronger balance sheet and momentum.
Tin is the tightest metal on the board (Myanmar broken, AI solder demand, $53K). Alphamin = ~7% of world supply, +10% over 200DMA. DRC risk = keep small.

tradeList.why: fabricator benefits from falling LME — wrong short
copper gap: long-bias commodity, tariff catalyst, 12/15 buys
tariff-losing steel replaces covered mill shorts
tin tightness pure-play, if TSXV accessible

sizingNote: NAV ~$10,950. Rules used: single name ≤ 8% NAV (~$875), commodity group ≤ 20% NAV, keep gross ≤ 75%. Current book: gross ~69%, net ~-31%. After the trade list: gross ~64%, net ~-14% — the deliberate effect is cutting the net short because the three strongest commodity biases (steel, copper, rare earths) are LONG while the shorts stay concentrated in the confirmed-weak complexes (PGM, silver, aluminum smelters).
